key_insight: A domain-decomposed randomized neural network splits unbounded PDEs into near-field and far-field regions, letting each subnetwork specialize while only linear output coefficients are solved.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical mesh-free alternative for exterior-domain simulations where artificial boundaries and global bases are hard to tune, especially for complex geometry and mixed local/far-field behavior.
method: Assign separate randomized subnetworks to different spatial regimes (near field vs far field).
Enforce coupling through boundary/interface conditions and solve only the last-layer coefficients via linear least squares.
Use Petrov--Galerkin for semi-unbounded elliptic problems and collocation for fully unbounded, perforated, and time-dependent problems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on Poisson and time-dependent Schrödinger equations, plus a bounded-parameter approximation result and an error decomposition.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the domain split, random features, and quadrature/collocation choices are tuned for the specific PDE and geometry.
paper_title: Domain-Decomposed Randomized Neural Networks for Partial Differential Equations in Unbounded Domains
